Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Leanstral: Open-Source foundation for trustworthy vibe-coding 209 upvotes, 36 comments
  2. My Journey to a reliable and enjoyable locally hosted voice assistant (2025) 296 upvotes, 92 comments
  3. Lazycut: A simple terminal video trimmer using FFmpeg 138 upvotes, 44 comments
  4. MoD sources warn Palantir role at heart of government is threat to UK security 569 upvotes, 230 comments
  5. Meta’s renewed commitment to jemalloc 297 upvotes, 125 comments
  6. The “small web” is bigger than you might think 270 upvotes, 118 comments
  7. Why I love FreeBSD 318 upvotes, 144 comments
  8. Comparing Python Type Checkers: Typing Spec Conformance 87 upvotes, 33 comments
  9. Corruption erodes social trust more in democracies than in autocracies 625 upvotes, 321 comments
  10. Language Model Teams as Distrbuted Systems 59 upvotes, 23 comments
  11. Home Assistant waters my plants 240 upvotes, 124 comments
  12. Lies I was told about collaborative editing, Part 2: Why we don't use Yjs 183 upvotes, 95 comments
  13. Launch HN: Chamber (YC W26) – An AI Teammate for GPU Infrastructure 19 upvotes, 5 comments
  14. On The Need For Understanding 63 upvotes, 28 comments
  15. Polymarket gamblers threaten to kill me over Iran missile story 1248 upvotes, 831 comments
  16. Speed at the cost of quality: Study of use of Cursor AI in open source projects (2025) 85 upvotes, 45 comments
  17. Kona EV Hacking 111 upvotes, 63 comments
  18. Show HN: Claude Code skills that build complete Godot games 122 upvotes, 71 comments
  19. US commercial insurers pay 254% of Medicare for the same hospital procedures 84 upvotes, 48 comments
  20. Show HN: Thermal Receipt Printers – Markdown and Web UI 7 upvotes, 1 comments
  21. Launch HN: Voygr (YC W26) – A better maps API for agents and AI apps 58 upvotes, 34 comments
  22. Nvidia Launches Vera CPU, Purpose-Built for Agentic AI 96 upvotes, 63 comments
  23. US Job Market Visualizer 381 upvotes, 302 comments
  24. Starlink Mini as a failover 163 upvotes, 144 comments
  25. Show HN: Oxyde – Pydantic-native async ORM with a Rust core 27 upvotes, 18 comments
  26. Apideck CLI – An AI-agent interface with much lower context consumption than MCP 112 upvotes, 103 comments
  27. The bureaucracy blocking the chance at a cure 77 upvotes, 108 comments
  28. AirPods Max 2 170 upvotes, 322 comments
